txt: Webbe, Erasmus, sometime of Gloucester Hall, fellow All Souls' Coll. 1567-83, from Cam, co. Gloucester; B.A. 31 May, 1568, M.A. 1 Dec., 1572, supd. for licence to preach 28 Nov., 1581, B.D. 21 June, 1585; vicar of St. Cleare, co. Carmarthen, 1577, rector of Ham, Wilts, 1582, and of Bletchingdon, Oxon, 1583, archdeacon of Buckingham 1589, canon of Windsor 1590, and rector of West Ilsley, Berks, 1601, until his death 24 March 1613. See Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 227; Fasti, i. 230; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Hurste, Charles, of co. Gloucester, gen. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19; rector of Hampton Meysey 1580, of Hawling 1584, and of Whittington (all co. Gloucester), 1606. See also Henry 1578, Nathaniel 1575, and Paris 1589. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Talbot, (Sir) John, of Ireland, gen. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20; of Castlering, co. Louth (s. William, of Malahide), knighted by Robert, earl of Essex, 6 Sept., 1599; died 7 Nov., 1613; brother of Walter same date, and perhaps of Henry.

Michell, Gilbert, of Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18; of Bodmin (s. Ralph), bar.-at-law, Lïncoln's Inn, 1584, M.P. Bodmin 1584-5; died 28 March, 1615; perhaps father of Ralph. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Binkes, William (Binxe), of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17; B.A. 8 Feb., 1577-8, one of these names rector of St. Andrew, Worcester, 1585. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hurste, Nathaniel, of co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17; B.A. 4 Feb., 1584-5, rector of Hawling, co. Gloucester, 1606. See Charles 1575, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stephens, Walter (Steavens), of co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 24, B.A. 3 March, 1574-5, M.A. May, 1580; rector of Bishops Castle, Salop, father of Jeremy.

Underhill, Nicholas, of co. Warwick, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 23, B.A. supd. Oct., 1573; vicar of Whitchurch, co. Warwick, and brother of Humphrey same date.
Plunket, Edward, of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20; student of Middle Temple 1580, as late of Furnival's Inn, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Underhill, Humphrey, of co. Warwick, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16; probably 2nd son of John, of Whitchurch, co. Warwick; brother of Nicholas same date.
Crispe, Henry, of Kent, 'eq. fil.' Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, son of Sir Henry, of Queekes, knight, brother of Edward same date, father of Nicholas 1626.
Vaughan, Rowland, of Wales, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 14 (erased in Mat. Reg.), "an excellent linguist and a renowned poet." See Ath. iii. 41.
Welbie, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 13; student of Lincoln's Inn 1580. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Evans, Lewis (Evanne), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 28; curate of Cassington, Oxon, 1580. See Ath. i. 412.
Talbot, Walter, of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 21; brother of John, same date and perhaps of Henry.
Lane, Hugh, of Bucks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 23; B.A. 25 June, 1575, M.A. 17 June, 1578.
Talbot, Henry, of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20. See John and Walter same date.
Canninge, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18; perhaps of Foxcote.
Smithe, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19, B.A. 17 Dec., 1576.
Crispe, Edward, of Kent, 'eq. fil.' Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, brother of Henry same date.
Clipsam, Edward (Clipson), of Northants, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Blower, Ralph (Blore), of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 24.
Garlicke, Nicholas, of co. Derby, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20.
Galway, Christopher, of Ireland, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19.
Raven, Philip, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 22.
Prendergaste, (—), of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 22.
Sury, John (or Smy), of Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19.
Edon, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16.
Boothe, George, of Cheshire, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19.
Fathers, Pancras, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan. 1574-5, aged 19.
Colman, Nicholas, of Kent, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19.
Dennys, William, of Devon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 17.
Martill, John, of Ireland, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20.
Bonny, Edward, of Sussex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16.
Whitty, (—), of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 21.
Gifforde, Thomas, of Bucks, Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16.
Pickins, Anthony, of co. Worcester. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5.

Tipping, (Sir) George, of Bucks, gen. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; of Whitfield and Draycott, Oxon, (son of Thomas, of Draycott); knighted 30 July, 1605; buried at Whitfield 4 May, 1627; brother of Bartholomew, father of John 1604, and of Samuel 1615.

Hele, William (or Pele), of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 1577, aged 23; B.A. from New Coll. 12 Feb., 1579-80, M.A. 4 July, 1582; perhaps rector of Lidford, Devon, 1589, vicar of St. Minver, Cornwall, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lewes, Thomas, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1586, as son and heir of William, of Monmouth, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Vyner, Henry, of Salop, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20; perhaps a student of Middle Temple 1579, as son and heir of Henry, of London, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hughes, Lewis (Hewes), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 22; vicar of Devynnock, co. Brecon, 1581, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tipping, Bartholomew, of Bucks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 14; son of Thomas, of Draycott, and father of the next; brother of George 1577.
Smith, John (Smyth), of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Thomas, Evan, co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18, B.A. 7 Feb., 1577-8; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 8 July, 1584.
Jones, David (Johns), of co. Glam. pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Booth, Thomas (Bouth), of Berks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Johnes, Thomas, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 24.
Booth, Thomas (Bothe), of co. Derby, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 1577, aged 14.
Walshe, Thomas, of co. Hereford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 1577, aged 15.
Chin, William, of co. Hereford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 1577, aged 29.
Williams, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 1577, aged 15.
Smith, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 1577, aged 17.
Heron, Edward, of London, gen. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 1577, aged 16.

Nowell, John, of Sussex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 8 Sept., 1578, aged 24, M.A. 1579; D.Med. of Leyden University; incorporated 27 June, 1585; of Trinity Minories, London, licenced 17 Feb., 1586-7, to marry Katherine, daughter of James Verseline, of the city of London, foreign merchant. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Long, Henry, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 12; student of Lincoln's Inn 1582 (as of Trowbridge, Wilts), probably of Whaddon and Smethwick, Wilts, and of Priston, Somerset (son of Henry, of Whaddon); born 18 April, 1564; his will dated 1612; father of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Fortescue, William, of Bucks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 16; of Salden, Bucks, perhaps a student of Inner Temple 1581 (as of London), M.P. Sudbury 1593, 1597-8, Stockbridge 1604-11, knighted at Drogheda 17 Nov., 1600, by Sir C. Blount, lord deputy. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Vowell, William (Vouell), of co. Pembroke, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 19, B.A. 14 Feb., 1581-2; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 6 Dec., 1585, sup. for licence to practice medicine July, 1588, as "Voel" or "Voyall"; perhaps rector of Gunton, Norfolk, 1581. See Foster's Index Eccl.

White, Richard (Whyte), of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 21; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 7 Nov., 1587, clerk 1589-91, chaplain 1591-9, M.A. 27 May, 1595; preacher at Horspath, 1592, 1597, and 1598. See Bloxam, ii. 46.

Radclyff, Charles (Ratclyff), of co. Durham, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date circa 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17; son of Christopher, of Newton Hansard, co. Durham, who recorded. his pedigree at the herald's visitation in 1575, Foster's edition.

Hurste, Henry, of co. Gloucester, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 14; perhaps B.A. 21 May, 1585, rector of Bourton-on-the-Water, co. Gloucester, 1590. See Charles 1575, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fortescue, Thomas, of Oxon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 12; possibly student of Inner Temple 1586, as of Salden, Bucks, gent. (son Sir John), M.P. Wycombe 1593. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Withers, Thomas (Wythers), of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 18, B.A. 7 Feb., 1582-3; vicar of Keevil 1585, and rector of Oaksey, (both) Wilts, 1595. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Foster, Richard, of Berks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.) 1578, aged 18; one of these names beneficed in Lincolnshire; probably brother of Pexall 1585. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dormer, William (Dermore), of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date (3 Aug.) 1578, aged 14; B.A. 27 June, 1584, of Lincoln's Inn 1589, formerly of Thavies' Inn. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Clarke, Edward (Clarcke), of Berks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date (3 Aug.) 1578, aged 19; probably a knight, and father either of Edward and John of 1625, or of John and Thomas 1627.
Bennett, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 2 April, 1582. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Strellye, Francis, of Notts, eq. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date circa 3 Aug., 1578, aged 14, 5th son of Sir Anthony, of Strelley, Notts, and brother of the next.
Lorte, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 19; B.A. 14 Feb., 1581-2, M.A. 3 June, 1584; admon. at Oxford 24 Nov., 1596.
Nedham, Thomas (Needam), of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 27; student of Gray's Inn 1580. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Stone, Robert, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17, B.A. 24 Oct., 1582; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 28 June, 1587.
Gethin, Maurice, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 20; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 29 Feb., 1583-4.
Gethin, Ellis, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 18; B.A. from Jesus Coll., 28 Feb., 1583-4.
Strellye, George, of Notts, eq. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date circa 3 Aug., 1578, aged 15; brother of the last-named.
Warren, William (Warryn), of co. Pembroke, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17.
Sandford, Michael, of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date circa 3 Aug., 1578, aged 19.
Draycott, John (Dreycott), of Ireland, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 19.
Collins, Nicholas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date (3 Aug.) 1578, aged 18.
Richardson, Conon, of co. Worcester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 15.
Curtis, Richard (Curtyse), of Wilts, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric.-entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 20.
Haworthe, Richard, of co. Hereford, gen. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 18.
Reason, Benjamin, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date circa 3 Aug., 1578, aged 16.
Willoughbye, William, of Dorset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17.
Yernall, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 20.
Widmerpoole, Robert, of Notts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 18.
Francke, George, of Yorks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date (3 Aug.), 1578, aged 19.
Patsolde, Walter, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 14.
Upperton, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 14.
Forde, John, of Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 18.

Turberveele, Richard, of Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 16; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 9 Feb., 1585-6; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 27 June, 1589; rector of Marcross, co. Glamorgan, 1597, canon of Llandaff 1598, vicar of Cardiff, co. Glamorgan, 1598, rector of Llanvigan, co. Brecon, 1601, precentor of collegiate church of Brecon 1615, canon of St. Davids 1615; perhaps brother of William 1587. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Evans, Lewis, of co. Monmouth, verbi minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1581, aged 31; B.A. 23 March, 1582-3, M.A. 8 July, 1585, perhaps rector of Machen, co. Monmouth, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Swetnam, Laurence, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1584, as son and heir of Laurence, of Sherborne, Dorset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wroughton, (Sir) Giles, of Wilts, equitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 17, "scholaris Mri. Case;" of Broad Hinton, Wilts (2s. Sir Thomas); brother of George 1586, and Thomas 1585.
Colman, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 21; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 26 Nov., 1584, rector of Thorpejuxta-Newark 1591. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Winter, Lewis (Wynter), of Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 19; student of Inner Temple 1591, as of Llangoed, co. Brecon, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hodgsone, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 15; B.A. 23 Jan., 1587-8, M.A. 5 Feb., 1593-4, licenced to practice medicine 8 Feb., 1596-7.
Bayly, Christopher, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1589. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Wellington, James, of co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Erington, Anthony, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1581-2 aged 16 (5s Gerard, of Heels, Wilts); "capitanus cæsus in Hibernia."
Collins, John (Collyns), of co. Stafford, 'serviens.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 16; one of these names B.A. 22 June, 1594.
Boothe, John, of co. Derby, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 21 (? B.A. from Hart Hall 13 May, 1585, as last entry).
Dowse, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 19; of Lincoln's Inn 1587. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Evan, David, of co. Radnor. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18; "serviens," perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 9 May, 1586.
Hodsden, John, of Middlesex, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 March, 1581-2, aged 19; called " Hodgsden," in Mat. Reg.
Slaughter, Thomas (Sloughter), of co. Gloucester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Hinton, Richard (Hynton), of co. Pembroke, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 23.
Bassett, Morgan, of co. Glamorgan, 'cler. fil.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 22.
Banester, Richard, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1581-2, aged 18.
Mor(r)ice, Edward, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1581-2, aged 15.
Phillips, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Mundy, Francis (Mondaye), of Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Turbe(r)vile, George, of Dorset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 18.
Meredyth, Thomas, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 23.
Slater, Edmund, of co. Lincoln, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Jan., 1581-2, aged 19.
Burchyll, Edward, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 20.
Morganes, John, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1581-2, aged 15.
Revell, Edward, of co. Derby, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Jan., 1581-2, aged 19.
Powell, Hugh, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 18.
Lewis, James, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Warde, John, of Middlesex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 March, 1581-2, aged 19.
Comley, Henry, of co. Hereford. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1581, aged 18.

Taylor, Henry (Tailor), of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 20, B.A. 27 June, 1584, M.A. 1 July, 1587; one of these names vicar of Stanton Harcourt, Oxon, 1590, another vicar of Bedfont, Middlesex, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jeffrey, Richard (Jefferaye), of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 15; perhaps demy Magdalen Coll. 1585-7, B.A. 27 June, 1587, fellow 1587-91, M.A. 10 July, 1590, canon of Sarum, 1590. See Bloxam, iv. 225; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bosvile, Edward (Bossevile), of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1582-3, aged 18; student of the Middle Temple 1585, as 4th son of Ralph, of Bradborne, Kent, esquire, deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Yorkshire Collection.

Salvin, Gerard, of co. Durham, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 9 Nov., 1582, aged 14; entered in Mat. Reg. as "Jarrard Salvey, of Doromnes"; a fair specimen of the Durham vernacular (s. Gerard, of Croxdale); buried 19 Sept., 1602.

Walter, Thomas, of Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 March, 1582-3, aged 18; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 15 Feb., 1586-7; perhaps vicar of Llandeloy and Llanhowell, co. Pembroke, 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chamberlayne, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, eq. aur. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 May, 1582, aged 14; perhaps of Oddington, co. Gloucester, brother of Edward and John 1579.
Francke, John (minimus), of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 12; one of these names M.P. Wareham 1597-8. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Rokebye, Ralph, of Yorks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1585. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Hovenden, Giles, of Leix, King's County, Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1582, aged 26; B.A. from Hart Hall 30 Jan., 1586-7.
Tipping, Richard (Typpinge), of Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 19 Oct., 1582, aged "8"; brother of Bartholomew and George 1577.
Bird, William, of Ireland, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 Jan., 1582-3, aged 18; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 9 Feb., 1584-5.
Wilson, William (Wylson), of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1582-3, aged 17, B.A. 28 Nov., 1586.
Franck, John, junior, of Dorset, pleb. fil. nat. min. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov. 1582, aged 19.
Burman, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1582-3, aged 17.
Gough, Walter, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 20.

Tomkins, James, of co. Hereford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 8 Nov., 1583, aged 14; of Monington, co. Hereford, student of Middle Temple 1589 (as son and heir of Richard, of Monnington, co. Hereford, esq.), M.P. Leominster 1624-5, 1625, 1626, and 1628-9, brother of Arthur, and father of John 1621, and Richard 1611. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Wilkins, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 June, 1583, aged 20, B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 23 Feb., 1586-7; rector of Wallingford St. Leonard 1592, and of Sulhamstead, (both) Berks, 1597. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Le(i)gh, Barnabie, of Cornwall, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1583-4, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1589, as son and heir of William, of St. Catherine's, Cornwall, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Underhill, George, of co. Warwick, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 15; of Oxhill, co. Warwick (s. Thomas, of Eatington); died in 1650; brother of John 1588, and of Edward 1587.
Price, Edward (Pryce), of co. Montgomery, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 July, 1583, aged 22.
Sedgrave, Christopher, of Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 15.
Deakin, Nicholas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 15.
Johnes, Edward, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 17.
Woodroffe, Robert, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 8 Nov., 1583, aged 10.
Persall, John, of Bucks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 11 Oct., 1583, aged 12.

Rogers, Thomas, of Dorset, eq. aur. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry dated 9 Oct., 1584, aged 10; perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 26 Feb., 1589-90, student of Middle Temple 1591, as y.s. Richard, of Bryanstone, Dorset, kt.; perhaps brother of William 1579. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Meredith, John, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 March, 1584, aged 19; B.A. 11 March, 1587-8; rector of Llandegla, co. Denbigh, 1606, and vicar of Whitford, co. Flints, 1608, and rector of Caerwys, Flints, 1617. See Thomas, St. Asaph, 618; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rogers, John, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Jan., 1584-5, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 28 Jan., 1584-5, M.A. 22 May, 1590, rector of Llanelien, 1598, and vicar of Devynnock, co. Brecon, 1615. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Davies, Hugh, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Oct., 1584, aged 16; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 12 Dec., 1588, perhaps rector of Derwen-y-mall, co. Denbigh, 1595. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Edmonds, John (Edmounds), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 20; perhaps a student of Gray's Inn 1584 from Clement's Inn. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Edmondeson, Jeffray, of co. Derby, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1585, as of Sawley, co. Derby. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Welcom, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1586, as of Lincoln city. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Evans, Lewis, of Radnor, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1584, aged 16; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 15 Feb., 1586-7; called also Lewis Evans alias Thomas.
Price, Robert, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1584-5, aged 18, B.A. 4 July, 1588; M.A. from Christ Church 15 April, 1592.
'Cardoinus', 'Camillus,' 'Baronis fil.,' born at Naples, Italy. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1584, aged 40.
Panton, Henry, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 18, B.A. 10 May, 1593.
Moore, Nicholas (Moare), of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1584-5, aged 32.
Rutland, William, of Sorreesis (Surrey), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 18.
Peirs, William (Piers), of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 July, 1584, aged 18.
Williams, William, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Jan., 1584-5, aged 17.
Myddelton, William, of co. Denbigh, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1584, aged 15.
Butler, Thomas, of Berks, 'cler. fil.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 March, 1584, aged 38.
Scull, Roger, of co. Hereford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 July, 1584, aged 20.
Polden, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 19.
Preston, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 19; one of these names rector of Notgrove, co. Gloucester, 1596. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Foster, Pexall, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 17; probably brother of Richard 1578.
Bates, Thomas, of Northumberland, 'gen. cond.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 March, 1585-6, aged 15.
Frith, Rowland (Frythe), of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1585, aged 16.
Hayne, John (Haine), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1585, aged 20.
Parry, Thomas, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 15 Oct., 1585, aged 13.
Panton, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 2 July, 1585, aged 14.
Parry, John, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 15 Oct., 1585, aged 14.
Bartrame, George, of Yorks, 'Gen. Cond.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1585, aged 20.
Bonell, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15.
Nippred, William, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1585, aged 15.
Adis, Henry, of Dorset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 25.

Catesbye, Robert, of co. Warwick, militis. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1586, aged 14 (2nd son of Sir William, of Lapworth); the noted recusant and adherent of Robert, Earl of Essex, and projector of the Gunpowder Plot, shot dead at Holbeach, co. Stafford, 8 Nov., 1605, brother of William 1586. See D.N.B. & Genealogist, v. 61.

Lawsone, Ralph, of co. Durham, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Feb., 1586-7, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1592, as of Nesham, Yorks, perhaps knighted 23 July, 1603, and died 6 Oct., 1623. See Raine's North Durham, 237; Foley's Records S.J. v. 708; & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Golloppe, Gibbes, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 Nov., 1586, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 5 June, 1589; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 21 June, 1592, rector of Odcombe, Somerset, 1607, and of Sutton Bingham 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.

'Barrker', John, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 17; B.A. from Balliol Coll., matric. 6 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 3 March, 1592-3, canon of Llandaff 1597. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Le Neve, ii. 265.
Saywell, William, of London (?) gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 21 Oct., 1586, aged 14; B.A. from Christ Church, 9 June, 1591, M.A. 26 June, 1594; admon. at Oxford 27 April, 1601.
Lingen, John, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1586-7, aged 27; "Iste dicebat se futurum ex aula Glocestrensi"; possibly son of Richard, of Stoke Edith, co. Hereford.
Catesbye, William, of co. Warwick, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1586, aged 15; brother of Robert.
Wolfe, Thomas (Wolphe), of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 35.
Hall, Thomas, of co. Derby, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Oct., 1586, aged 19.
Shirley, Thomas (Shurley), of co. Leicester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Feb., 1587-8, aged 16; 3rd son of John, of Staunton Harold, co. Leicester, and brother of George 1573, and of John 1582.
Turbervill, William, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 June, 1587, aged 18, B.A. 16 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 17 March, 1592-3, B.C.L. supd. 19 Oct., 1596; perhaps brother of Richard 1581.
Constable, Ralph, of co. York, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 June, 1587, aged 18; possibly an uncle of Henry, Viscount Dunbar. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.
Smith, Francis (Smyth), of co. Warwick, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1587, aged 15; possibly brother of George 1589.
Harris, Francis, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1587, aged 17.
Watkins, Harry, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Feb., 1587-8, aged 19.
Kinge, William, of Beds, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 April, 1587, aged 20.
Coxe, Roger, of Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 April, 1587, aged 19.
Lloyd, Robert, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1588, aged 20; perhaps B.A. from Hart Hall 4 Feb., 1591-2, as Floyd.
Roper, Philip, of Kent, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Sept., 1588, aged 15 (subscribes Rooper).
Draycote, Philip, of co. Derby, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Oct., 1588, aged 14.

Bowne, Edward (Boun), of Notts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1589-90, aged 15; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1592, specially admitted because his father and his grandfather were of that society. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Smith, George (Smyth), of co. Leicester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 27 Nov., 1589, aged 13; perhaps of Queenborough, co. Leicester (son of George, of Ashby Folville); died in 1619; probably brother of Francis 1587.

Percy, William, born in the bishoprick of Durham, comitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 June, 1589, aged 15 (3s. Henry, 2nd earl of Northumberland); buried in Christ Church cathedral 28 May, 1648; brother of Alan, etc.
Hudson, Samuel, of co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 March, 1589-90, aged 17; B.A. 17 Dec., 1597, as "Hodson."
Hancocke, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Oct., 1589, aged 19; B.A. 2 July, 1593.
Hurst, Paris, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Nov., 1589, aged 18. See Charles 1575, etc.
Sexton, Edmond, of "Co." Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Jan., 1589-90, aged 20.
Wigges, Alexander, of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 30 May, 1589, aged 14.
Smithe, John (Smythe), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Oct., 1589, aged 25.
Archedale, Abraham, of Oxon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Feb., 1589-90, aged 25.
Smith, Valentine, of Essex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1589-90, aged 15.
Kempe, Henry, of Sussex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1589-90, aged 15.
Browne, Anthony, of Sussex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 15.

Bishopp, Roger, of co. Warwick, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 July, 1590, aged 16; a student of the Inner Temple 1593, as of Gt. Wolford, co. Warwick, late of Clement's Inn, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lloid, William, of Salop, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 March, 1590-1, aged 21; perhaps bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1600, as of Bettws, Salop, and as Floyd, als. Lloyd. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Orrell, Peter, of co, Lancaster, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 5 June, 1590, aged 13 (?23).
Moore, William (Moare), of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1590-1, aged 15.
Rouse, John (Rowse), of Northants, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1590-1, aged 17.

Copley, William (Coppley), of co. Worcester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 March, 1591-2, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1611, as 4th son of Thomas, of Breedon, co. Worcester, esq., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

South, Edward, of co. Lincoln, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1595, as son and heir of Edward, late of Kerrington, co. Lincoln, gent., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hawkins, (Sir) Thomas, of Kent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 15 (as gent.); of Nash Court, Kent (1s. Sir Thomas, knight banneret); knighted at Whitehall 4 May, 1618; died in 1640. See Dicty. Natl. Biography.

Berney, John (Barney), of Norfolk, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 July, 1591, aged 11; student of Lincoln's Inn 1598, a younger brother of Sir Thomas Berney, knight, and of the last named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Tasker, Thomas, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 12 June, 1591, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1595, as son of Roger, of London, goldsmith. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Berney, Henry (Barney), of Norfolk, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 July, 1591, aged 13; probably a son of Henry Berney, of Park Hill, in Reedham, Norfolk.
Rucke, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1595, as of London. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Alderson, John, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 June, 1591, aged 15; (? bar.at-law Lincoln's Inn 1603). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hinde, Thomas (Hynde), of Kent, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 March, 1591-2, aged 16; student of Lincoln's Inn 1594. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hawkines, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1591, aged 19; sup. for B.A. 16 March, 1593-4, then of St. Alban Hall.
Powell, William, of Kent, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 14; perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 24 Nov., 1596.
Phillipps, Walter, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. 20 April, 1594.
Hyde, Thomas, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1591, aged 14. See Leonard, 1571.
Carter, Edward, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Jan., 1591-2, aged 17.
Lu(c)kyn, Edward, of Essex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1591, aged 24.
Barbare, John, of Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1591, aged 13.

Gwyn, Owen, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 16; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 6 July, 1598, M.A. 10 July, 1607; perhaps vicar of East Ham, Essex, 1605-11, rector of South Luffenham, Rutland, 1611-13, canon of Lincoln 1622, archdeacon of Huntingdon 1622, until his death in 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Kemys, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 June, 1592, aged 16; B.A. 17 Feb., 1595-6; possibly a student of Middle Temple 1589, as 2nd son of Robert, of Cucklington, Somerset, gent., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Llewelyn, Hugh (Llawelyn), of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 17.
Wedgwood, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 June, 1592, aged 20.
Hill, Charles, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 June, 1592, aged 13.
Hawkins, Henry, of Kent, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1592, aged 14.

Percy, (Sir) Alan, of Sussex, comitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Jan., 1593-4, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1597, as 5s. Henry, and brother of Henry, late Earls of Northumberland, baptised 2 July, 1577, k.b. 1604, admon. granted 15 June, 1613, "late of Bolton, co. Cumberland, and dying in the parish of St. Clement Danes;" brother of George, and Josseline 1594, and Thomas 1581, and William 1589. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Percy, (Sir) Josseline, of Sussex, comitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 11 Jan., 1593-4, aged 14; "the noted wit," knighted by Robert, Earl of Essex, 30 July, 1599, after the fight of Offaly, in Ireland; admon. granted 28 May, 1631, "late of the precincts of the Tower of London, bachelor, deceased;" brother of Alan, etc. See Rawl. xvii. 160.
Reynold, Richard (Rainold), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 4 May, 1593, aged 13.
Butler, William, of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Aug., 1593, aged 10.
Butler, Dudley, of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Aug., 1593, aged 18.
Butler, Edmund, of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Aug., 1593, aged 14.
Butler, Roger, of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Aug., 1593, aged 15.
Mill, Joseph, of Northants, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Aug., 1593, aged 12.

Prichard, Thomas (Prychard), of co. Glamorgan, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 17, and again 5 March, 1595-6, aged 18, pleb. B.A. 22 May, 1599; rector of Michaelston alias Llanmihangel-juxta-Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, 1617, archdeacon of Llandaff 1626, canon of Hereford 1636; died May, 1646. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Twisse, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 18, B.A. 17 Dec., 1597, M.A. 9 July, 1602; rector of Burwardscott, Berks, 1614; father of John 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cooper, Thomas, of Oxon, Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 20; B.A. from Christ Church 30 Oct., 1598, M.A. from St. Mary Hall 3 July, 1602.
Parsons, Thomas, of co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from Merton Coll. 8 April, 1600.
Parry, Roger, of co. Brecon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 16; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 22 June, 1598.
Price, Richard (Pryce), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6.
Comley, John (Comeley), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall., matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 19.
Hancock, Hugh (Hancoke), of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 13.
Morgans, Howell, of co. Brecon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 18.
Powell, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 16.
Creed, William, of Somerset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 20.
Richardsone, John, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 17.
Evans, David, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 16.
Hughes, John, of co. Flint, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 18.
Dawson, Edward, of Essex, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 17.
Lawsonn, Edmund, of Yorks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1595, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1599, as son of Ralph, of Brough, Yorkshire, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Williams, Rees, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 March, 1595-6, aged 15; perhaps student of Lincoln's Inn 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Clarke, Josias, of Dorset, 'cler. fil.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 March, 1595-6, aged 16; B.A. 31 Jan., 1599-1600, M.A. 6 July, 1603.
Rogers, John, of co. Brecon, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 March, 1595-6, aged 18.
Hobbs, Stephen, of Wilts, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 March, 1595-6, aged 25.
Evered, John, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 March, 1595-6, aged 16.
Coryate, Thomas, s. (George), of Somerset, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 June, 1596, aged 19; an Eastern traveller, died at Surat Dec., 1617. See Ath., ii. 208; & D.N.B.
Meade, Edmund, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Feb., 1596-7, aged 17; B.A. 11 Dec., 1600, rector of Pitney Lorty, Somerset, 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynolds, William (Raynolde), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 14 Oct., 1596, aged 14.

Winston, Giles (Wynson), of co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597, aged 18, student of Inner Temple 1599, as of Whittington Court, co. Gloucester, gent.; perhaps son of Sir Henry, of Standish. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Dewell, Henry, of Middlesex, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 9 July, 1600, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1608, autumn reader elect 1625. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Elye, Nicholas, of Hants, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. Jan. or Feb., 1597-8, aged 17; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 19 June, 1601, M.A. 27 June, 1604, rector of Winterslow, Wilts, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Badger, Augustine, of co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 April, 1597, aged 45. See Bigland's Gloucestershire, ii. 323.
Bishop, John (Bisshopp), of co. Stafford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597. aged 17.
Hodges, John, of Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597, aged 19.
Johnes, Robert, of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597, aged 14.
Bale, John, of Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 June, 1597, aged 17.

Marmion, Edmund, of Northants, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 March, 1598-9, aged 15; B.A. 17 Dec., 1602, M.A. 30 June, 1606, baptised at Aynho 21 July, 1583, rector of Eynesbury, Hunts, 1614, until sequestered in 1645 by the Westminster assembly; brother of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MSS. 15,669-70.

Cheriton, Charles, of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1598, aged 18 (called Charington in Mat. Reg.); B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 28 June, 1604; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 27 June, 1607.
Gainsford, John (Gaynesford), of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Feb., 1598-9, aged 14; a student of Gray's Inn 1608, as son of William, of Ford, Surrey, kt. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Gwillam, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1598, aged 25.
Marshall, William, of Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Feb., 1598-9, aged 21.

Clarke, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 May, 1599, aged 17; B.A. 1 March, 1602-3, M.A. 4 July, 1608, rector of Haselbury Bryan, Dorset, 1612, father of Thomas 1632. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hamlyn, Francis, of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 17; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 16 Feb., 1603-4; "disp. towards" M.A. 3 June, 1606, from Broadgates Hall.
Chelmicke, Thomas, of Salop, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 May, 1599, aged 17.
Cressy, Robert, of Sussex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 15.
Coles, Peter, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1599, aged 26.

Wrothe, (Sir) Thomas, of London, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 July, 1600, aged 19; of Petherton Park, Somerset, student of Inner Temple 1607 (1s. Thomas), knighted 11 Nov., 1613, M.P. Bridgwater 1628-9 circa Oct., 1645-53 (L.P.), Oct., 1656-8, rumper 1659, 1660; one of the commissioners on trial of the king, but did not act; his will proved 24 Aug., 1672. See Ath. iii. 514; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Rumsey, Walter, of co. Monmouth, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn 1608, bencher 1634 (as of Usk, co. Monmouth, gent.), a justice in south Wales, called "the picklock of the law;" M.P. co. Monmouth, in the "little parliament" April-May, 1640; died about 1660, buried in Llanover church; father of Edward 1624. See Ath. iii. 509; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Dormer, (Sir) Robert, of Bucks. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 11 (as arm.); of the Inner Temple 1608, as of Dorton, Bucks, esq. (son and heir of Sir John), knighted 11 Sept. 1617, died 12 May, 1689, buried at Crendon, Bucks, brother of Giffard, and father of John 1637. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Johnes, Lewis, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1600, aged 19 (called "Lewis Lewis" in Mat. Reg.), B.A. 30 April, 1602, dispensed towards M.A. from St. Mary Hall 31 Oct., 1605, vicar of Llanddewy Rytherch, co. Monmouth, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Greenfeilde, Joseph, of Somerset, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1600, aged 18; B.A. 16 Feb., 1603-4, M.A. 4 July, 1608, rector of Whitstaunton, Somerset, 1615, and of Combe St. Nicholas 1617, until his death 1643; father of Thomas. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Sheldon, Ralph, of co. Worcester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry dated 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged "7"; of Steeple Barton, Oxon, son of Edward, of Beoley, co. Worcester, and brother of Edward 1621, and of William 1601. See Ath. i. p. xxv.

Fowlear, Edward, of co. Stafford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1600, aged 15; student of the Inner Temple 1606, as of St. Thomas, co. Stafford (s. Walter); died 1625; brother of Bryan. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hawley, Hatton, of Somerset, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1616 (as 2s. Francis, late of Purbeck island, Dorset, esq., deceased). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bell, Edmond, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 12; a student of the Middle Temple 1616, as son and heir of Xenophon, of Well, co. Worcester, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Dormer, Giffard, of Bucks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged '7'; of the Inner Temple 1608 as of Dorton, Bucks, gent. (2s. Sir John), brother of Robert 1601. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Sheldon, William, of co. Worcester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 11 (1s. Edward, of Beoley, co. Worcester); died 1659, brother of Ralph 1601, and of Edward 1621.
Moris, William, of co. Monmouth, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 13; student of Gray's Inn 1606, as of Creyollwy, co. Monmouth, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Russell, John, of co. Worcester, equitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry dated 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 13; perhaps son of Sir John, of Strensham. See Thomas 1588 and 1591.
Vachell, Thomas, of Berks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 7 Nov., 1600, aged 14; B.A. from Christ Church 27 June, 1605.
Moorthat, John (? Moorthwaite), of Cumberland, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 22.
Fettiplace, John, of co. Gloucester, equitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 13.
Morgan, Walter, of co, Monmouth, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 15.
Weldon, Francis, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 15.
Ball, Henry, of Devon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1600, aged 19.

Baskervile, Humfrey, of co. Hereford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 14; a student of the Inner Temple 1604, as of Eardisley, co. Hereford, knighted at Theobald's 7 March, 1608-9, died 3 April, 1647-8, father of George. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Monington, Richard, of co. Hereford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1604, as of Sarnsfield Water, co. Hereford (son of Thomas); died before 1645. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Coningsby, Thomas (Conyngesbye), of co. Hereford, eq. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 13 (son of Sir Thomas last named); buried at St. Thomas Church, Oxford, 22 May, 1602, brother of Fitzwilliam.

Whorwood, William, of co. Stafford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 July, 1601, aged 21; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1607, as of Sandwell Abbey, co. Stafford, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Huntley, George, of Surrey, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 July, 1601, aged 16; B.A. 3 July, 1605, M.A. 4 July, 1608, rector of Stourmouth, Kent, 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Underehill, Richard, of co. Warwick, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1601, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1607. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cheriton, Mathew, of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1601, aged 15; B.A. 10 June, 1608, M.A. 9 May, 1611.
Lownds, Thomas, of Bucks, "Servus Mri. Hawley, Principalis." Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 18.
Clarke, Roger, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 16; B.A. 27 Nov., 1606.
Randolphe, Charles, of co. Warwick, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 12 June, 1601, aged 13.
Hackluyt, Thomas, of co. Hereford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 16.
Benson, John, of Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 19.
Newman, Richard, of Dorset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 17; of Fifield, Dorset (s. Thomas); died 10 July, 1664; father of Richard, and of Thomas 1635.
Newton, John, of Salop, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 March, 1602-3, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gresley, William, of co. Stafford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 Jan., 1602-3, aged 19; (s. Sir Thomas), brother of Walsingham 1603, etc.
Gresley, Walsingham, of co. Stafford, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 Jan., 1602-3, aged 17; (s. Sir Thomas); brother of George 1594, etc.
Brooke, Christopher, of Sussex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1602, aged 17. See Thomas 1597.
Dynham, John, of Oxon, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1602, aged 15.
James, Abraham, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 19 (? 9).
Huyshe, James, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 April, 1602, aged 14.
Newton, Peter, of Salop, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 March, 1602-3, aged 17.
Newton, Thomas, of Devon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1602, aged 15.

White, Nicholas, of co. Meath, Ireland, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16; one of these names M.P. Clonmel 1613, another M.P. Maryborough 1639, until his death in 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Draycott, Henry, of co. Meath, Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 July, 1603, aged. 15.

Mo(o)re, John, of Surrey, equitis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1604-5, aged 15; B.A. 17 Dec., 1608, student of Inner Temple 1610 (4s. Sir George), born 17 May, 1589; brother of William 1601, and Robert 1595. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hawley, Edward (Hauly), of Somerset, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1604-5, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1608 (as 4th son of Francis, of Corfe Castle, Dorset, esq.); perhaps knighted 23 Nov., 1624. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hawley, Jerome, of Middlesex, arm., fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1609, as 2nd son of James, of Burston, Middlesex, esq.; brother of Richard 1608. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wrothe, Jerome, of Herts, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1609, as 2s. Richard, of Standon, Herts, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hord, Allen, of Surrey, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 15 (s. Alan, of Ewell, Surrey, and of Hord Park, Salop); will dated 2 July, 1609, proved 9 Nov. following.
Evans, Gawen, of Somerset, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 22; rector of West Quantoxhead, Somerset, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Higgins, John, of Wilts, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 19; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 27 June, 1607.
Prichard, Roderick, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 20; B.A. 3 July, 1605.
Balche, John, of Dorset. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 19; 'famulus Mri. Hawley principalis.'
Newings, Thomas, of Surrey, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 17; B.A. 8 Feb., 1604-5.
Dun, John, of Essex, militis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 June, 1605, aged 15; of Gray's Inn 1606, as son and heir of Daniel, knt., brother of Cæsar 1612. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Potman, Richard, of Kent, militis fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1605, aged 17; B.A. 17 Dec., 1608, M.A. 28 May, 1611, vicar of Wantage, Berks, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hurde, Edward, of Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1605, aged 17; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 23 Jan., 1609-10, M.A. 10 March, 1612-13, B.D. 26 June, 1622.
Wild, Edmund, of Essex, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 14 June, 1605, aged 14.
Kyrle, William, of co. Hereford. gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 July, 1605, aged 15.
Bunkar, Edward, of Bucks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 June, 1605, aged 15.

Lower, Alexander, of Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 April, 1606, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1610, as 5s. Thomas, of Winnowe, Cornwall, esq.; brother of Sir Nicholas and Sir William. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Greenfeild, Nathaniel (Grinfeild), of Somerset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 May, 1606, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 7 July, 1609, M.A. 5 March, 1612-13, preacher at Whitfield, Oxon. See Fasti, i. 347.
Saunders, Valentine, of Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 May, 1606, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1611, as of Chiswick, Middlesex, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Lu(c)kin, Thomas, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1606-7, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1617. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Rogers, Francis, of Surrey, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 16 Oct., 1607, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1609, as of London, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Napkyn, Thomas, of Oxford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1607, aged 17; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 21 Jan., 1612-13.

Handman, John, of co. Gloucester. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1609, aged 16, paup. schol., B.A. 12 May, 1613, M.A. 4 June, 1616; perhaps rector of Hartley Mauditt, Hants, &c., 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Welbecke, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1609-10, aged 17.
Power, Zacharias, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1609, aged 19.

Breton, Robert, of Northants, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1611, as son and heir of John, of Teton, Northants, esq., baptized at Ravensthorpe 9 April, 1592, buried there 17 Jan., 1640-1, father of Francis 1636. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Francke, William, of Devon, paup. schol. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 19; B.A. 26 Oct., 1613, M.A. 4 June, 1616, rector of Stockleigh English, Devon, 1615, and of Poughill, 1625, of Cruwys Morchard 1629, vicar of Gwinnear, Cornwall, 1636; father of William 1636 and of Abraham 1638.

Studley, Peter, of Salop, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Dec., 1610, aged 22, B.A. 30 June, 1614, M.A. 2 May, 1617; vicar of St. Chads, Shrewsbury, 1628-37, rector (2nd portion) Pontesbury, Salop, 1636; died 1648. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Tylor, William, of co. Gloucester, "paup. schol." Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 18, B.A. 9 Nov., 1612, M.A. 4 June, 1616; vicar of Bishop Nympton 1620, and of Witheridge, Devon, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Earle, Robert, of Somerset, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 20; B.A. 28 Jan., 1610-11, rector of Litton, Somerset, 1616, rector and preb. of St. Decumans in Wells, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Latton, John, of Oxon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1610, aged 13; possibly a student of the Middle Temple 1612, as son and heir of William, of Kingston Bagpuze, Berks, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Marriat, William, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry dated 12 Oct., 1610, aged 11; student Middle Temple 1617, as son and heir of John, of Remenham, Berks. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wollascott, Martin, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1613, as 2s. William, of Shinfield, Berks, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Young, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16; perhaps bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1628. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Kirkham, Anthony, of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16; B.A. 10 Feb., 1612-13, sup. for M.A. 22 Nov., 1617.
Wollascott, William, of Berks, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 20 (?); of Walhampton; brother of the preceding.
Winchcombe, William, of Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16.
Gerard, Thomas, of Somerset, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16.

Hawley, Richard, B.A. from Gloucester Hall, 10 June 1608; fellow Merton Coll. 1611, M.A. 27 June, 1615; incorporated D.Med. from Leyden 11 July, 1627; fellow college of physicians 1630; died 30 April, 1636; brother of Jerome and perhaps father of Henry 1646. See O.H.S. iv. 279; Fasti, i. 434; & Munk's Roll, i. 201.

Poole, Richard, of Salop, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Jan., 1611-12, aged 17, "paup. schol.," B.A. 24 Nov., 1615, M.A. 3 July, 1618, vicar of Brace Meole 1618, of Hanwood 1627, of St. Chads, Shrewsbury, 1637-44. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Sim(p)son, Martin, of Northants, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 April, 1615, aged 17, B.A. 27 April, 1615; student of Inner Temple 1614, as son and heir of Anthony, of Welford, Northants, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Calamy, ii. 219.
Williams, Christopher, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1615, aged 20 (famulus Dris. Harvey); perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 15 Dec., 1618, though doubtful.
Jeffreys, Roger (Jeffreis), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 June, 1615, aged 19.
Per(r)in, Samuel, of Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 June, 1616, aged 19, B.A. 21 June, 1620, rector of Littleham, Devon, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Atherton, John, s. (John), of Somerset, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Oct., 1617, aged 19, B.A. 27 Nov., 1617; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 11 June, 1621, rector of Huish Champflower, Somerset, 1622, until bishop of Waterford and Lismore 1636, preb. 1630-5, and chancellor of Christ Church, Dublin, 1635-9, chancellor of Killaloe 1634-6, on the perjured evidence of a single person (who was himself eventually executed) he was found guilty and hanged at Dublin, 5 Dec., 1645. See Cotton's Fasti Eccl. Hib., i. 127; & Ath., ii. 891.
Pochin, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 March, 1617, aged 20; B.A. 13 Dec., 1620, M.A. 18 June, 1623.
Harvey, John, of Middlesex, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 May, 1617, aged 17; B.A. 10 June, 1618.

Weston, Antony, of co. Stafford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1618, aged 18, B.A. 15 Dec., 1618, M.A. 12 June, 1621, elected to Christ Church from Westminster school 1614. See Al. West. 83.
Cæsar, Augustin, of Kent, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1618, aged 16; B.A. 30 Jan., 1621-2, D.Med. 10 Aug., 1660 (son of Martin), of Rochester, buried in Rochester Cathedral 1677, aged 75.
Hall, James, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 May, 1618, aged 20.
Fisher, Augustine, of Devon, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 June, 1619, aged 15.

Browne, Jonathan, of Herts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1620, aged 19; B.C.L. 3 Feb., 1624-5, D.C.L. 30 June, 1630, rector of Shelley, Essex, 1621, and of St. Faith's, London, 1628, sequestered, rector of Hertingfordbury 1630, canon of Hereford 1636, dean 1636-43, canon of Westminster 1639, died Dec., 1643. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, i. 456.

Stersaker, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1620, aged 21 (subs. "Styrsaker"), B.A. 28 Nov., 1620; one of these names vicar of Mountnessing, Essex, deprived 1503. See Newcourt.
Cosier, Christopher, of Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 June, 1620, aged 20; one of these names vicar of Catherington, Hants, 1660. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Pett, William, of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1620, aged 18, B.A. 6 June, 1621; rector of Cuxton, Kent, 1631. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wolston, John, of Southants, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 June, 1620, aged 19, B.A. 13 Dec., 1620.

Onslowe, Edward, of Salop, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 April, 1621, aged 19; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 21 Oct., 1624, M.A. 2 June, 1627, rector of Uley, co. Gloucester, 1639, canon of Sarum 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Randall, William (Randoll), of co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1632, as 2s. John, of Preston Baggot, co. Warwick, gen. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Siston, Joshua, of co. Leicester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 May, 1621, aged 17, B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4; rector of Adbolton, Notts, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Evan, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 April, 1621, aged 20; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 26 Jan., 1623-4.
Storre, Francis, of co. Lincoln, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 April, 1621, aged 17, B.A. 27 Nov., 1621.
Morgans, Hugh, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 20; B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4.
Roberts, John, of co. Monmouth, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 May, 1621, aged 20.
White, Gabriel (Whighte), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 May, 1621, aged 19.
Harris, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 19.
Wight, Gabriel (Whight), of Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 May, 1621, aged 19.
James, Lewis, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 May, 1621, aged 19.

Wells, Francis, s. Francis, of Mickleton, co. Gloucester, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 16, B.A. 20 Nov., 1626, M.A. 25 June, 1629; perhaps rector of Weston Turville, Bucks, 1645, and exchanged for vicarage of Adderbury, Oxon, 1646; brother of John 1627. See Add. MSS. 15,669-70-71.

Winston, Walter, s. Hugh, of Brill, co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 21, B.A. 13 Dec., 1625, M.A. 21 June, 1628; rector of Newchurch, Radnor, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Howard, Francis, s. John, of Cotsford, Northants, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 18; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 30 Jan., 1626-7, M.A. 14 June, 1632.
Humphrey, Rice, s. Rice, of Llanerch Medd, co. Anglesea, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 16; B.A. 14 Feb., 1627-8.
Edwards, Edmond, s. Elias, of Lidington, Wilts, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1622-3, aged 19; B.A. 20 Nov., 1626.
James, Thomas, s. John, of Stanton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 18.
Sheate, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Chisleton, Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 March, 1622-3, aged 18.

Sandys, William, 2s. William, of Flatbury, co. Worcester, militis. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 June, 1623, aged 16; of Miserden, co. Gloucester, called "Waterworks Sandys," student of Middle Temple 1626, licenced 24 April, 1633, to marry Cecily, daughter of Sir John Steed, of Steed Hill, Kent, kt.; brother of Miles and Thomas. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Whear, William, born in Dorset, s. Degory, of university of Oxford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 13 June, 1623, aged 13, B.A. 2 June, 1627; fellow Merton Coll. 1629, M.A. 1 July, 1633; buried in the choir of the college chapel 21 Sept., 1634. See Gutch, i. app. 207.

Sandys, Thomas, 3s. William, of Flatbury, co. Worcester, militis. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 June, 1623, aged 15, B.A. 21 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 3 June, 1630, B.C.L. 29 March, 1634; brother of Miles 1616.
Clark, Richard, of Worcester (city), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Oct., 1623, aged 40; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 22 June, 1626, M.A. 28 April, 1629. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bate, Walter, s. Richard, of Larrock, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 April, 1623, aged 18; B.A. from Hart Hall 31 Jan., 1626-7.
Carter, Edmond, s. 'Randol.' of Foston, co. Leicester. pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Feb., 1623-4, aged 18.
Pym, John (Pim), 1s. John, of Brymore, Somerset, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 13 June, 1623, aged 14.
Keene, Charles, s. John, of Miserden, co. Gloucr., pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 June, 1623, aged 18.

Palmer, Henry, s. Henry, of Milverton, co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 July, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 19 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 15 June, 1630; vicar of Lillington, co. Warwick, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Davis, (Sir) John, of Oxon, 1s. John, of Pangborne, Berks, militis. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1626, aged 15; (? B.A. 1 Feb., 1626-7) of Bere Court, Berks, admitted to the Middle Temple 1629, a justice of the peace for Berks, knighted 14 Nov., 1662. See Gregory 1689; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hardinge, John, s. Robert, of Bocconoc, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1626, aged 17; B.A. 16 Jan., 1629-30, rector of Boconnoc 1635, and of Llandulp, Cornwall, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rudyerde, Lawrence (Rudierde), 1s. Lawrence, of Winchfield, Southants, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1626, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1628; brother of James 1631. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cheshire, Edward, s. John, of Clipston, Northants, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1626-7, aged 18.

Widdowes, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Mickleton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 April, 1627, aged 14; demy Magdalen Coll. 1630-6, B.A. 11 Feb., 1630-1, M.A. 17 Dec., 1633; master of the college school at Gloucester. 1640, ejected, became minister of Woodstock and master of the school there, and finally at Northleach, co. Gloucester, where he died, buried there 26 June, 1655; brother of Giles. See Ath. iii. 398; & Bloxam, v. 127.

Welles, John, s. Francis, of Mickleton, co. Gloucester, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 April, 1627, aged 18, B.A. 28 Jan., 1629-30; vicar of Tewkesbury, co. Gloucester, ejected 1662 for nonconformity; brother of Francis 1623, and Michael 1640. See Calamy, ii. 254.

Orchard, John, s. John, of St. Mary Weeke, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 17; B.A. 3 Dec., 1633, M.A. 30 June, 1636; vicar of St. Kew, Cornwall, 1640, prebendary of Priors Bodmyn als Kings, prebendary in collegiate church of Endelian, Cornwall, 1661, father of Nicholas 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Halhed, Nathaniel, s. Thomas, of Banbury, Oxon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 June, 1629, aged 20; B.A. 25 June, 1629, M.A. 26 April, 1632, rector of Halhead, co. Worcester, 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 209.

Risdon, Anthony, s. Philip, of Buckland-Brewer, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 21, B.A. 20 Oct., 1632; M.A. from Trinity Coll. 4 June, 1635, vicar of Cubert, Cornwall, 1643. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Counsell, William, s. William, of Barrowe, Somerset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 20; B.A. 1 Feb., 1630-1, M.A. 28 Jan., 1633-4, rector of Marksbury, Somerset, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Harrison, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Morwenstow, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 20; B.A. 24 Jan., 1631-2, perhaps rector of Michaelstow and of Lanivett, Cornwall, 1630. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Orchard, William, s. John, of St. Mary Week, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 19; B.C.L. 17 June, 1634; perhaps vicar of St. Hilary, Cornwall, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Webber, William, s. John, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 18, B.A. 24 Jan., 1631-2, B.C.L. 29 March, 1634; student of Inner Temple 1632. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Watkins, Henry, s. Henry, of Wraxall, Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1629, aged 21, B.A. 10 Nov., 1629; rector of Caundle Bishops, Dorset, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Fitzgeffrye, Charles, s. Charles, of St. Dominick, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 19; B.A. 10 Nov., 1631; brother of John 1624.
Counsell, John, s. John, of Barrowe, Somerset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30; aged 18; B.Med. 30 June, 1636.
Sarsfield, William, s. Michael, of Bodmin, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 26.
Rodd, William, s. Nicholas, of Stratton, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1629-30, aged 20.

Bassett, Thomas, s. William, of Bonvilston, co. Glamorgan, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16; rector of Landough with Leckwith, co. Glamorgan, 1639, died in May 1666. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Prichard, George, s. George, of Llanmase, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 19; B.A. 9 May, 1633, M.A. 30 June, 1636, rector of Llansannor, co. Glamorgan, 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Griffeth, William, s. Maurice, of Stepney, Middlesex, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 17; "poore scholar," St. Paul's, 1626-30, exhibitioner Gloucester Hall 1629-33. See St. Paul's S.R. 36.

Eedes, John, s. John, of Honiton, Devon, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16; B.C.L. 17 Dec., 1635; his father rector of Honiton 1614-52. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 6.
Kitt, Henry, s. Andrew, of Stowford, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16; B.A. 18 Dec., 1634 (as Kitts), rector of Gulval, Cornwall, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Whear, John, born in Bucks, s. Degory, principal of Gloucester Hall. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 15, B.A. 3 Dec., 1633, M.A. 30 June, 1636.
Willis, John, s. Thomas, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16; perhaps B.A. from Wadham Coll. 2 Dec., 1637.
Martin, Abednego, s. Richard, of St. Marychurch, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 17; B.A. 28 Jan., 1633-4.
Bassett, William, s. William, of Cardiff, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 18; B.A. 20 Oct., 1630.
Congreve, Thomas, s. Francis, of Steaton, co. Stafford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16.
Dabbe, John, s. Richard, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 17; B.A. 3 Dec., 1633.
Rees, Cradocke, s. Walter, of Llanmase, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 16.
Early, Ferdinand, s. William, of Salisbury, Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1630, aged 19.
Millett, John, 1s. John, of Hayes, Middlesex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 17.
Birch, John, s. Richard, of Chesham, Bucks, pleb. Trnity Coll., matric. 13 May, 1631, aged 18; B.A. 4 May, 1632, M.A. from Gloucester Hall 15 April, 1635. See Foster's Inns of Court Admissions.
Brushe, William, s. Henry, of Manaccan, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Feb., 1631-2, aged 23; brother of George 1631.
Hill, James, sacerd. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 May, 1631, aged 20.

Merrett, Christopher, s. Christopher, of Winchcombe, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 June, 1632, aged 17; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 24 Jan., 1634-5; B.Med. from Gloucester Hall 30 June, 1636, created D.Med. 31 Jan., 1642-3, "an eminent physician," born at Winchcombe 16 Feb., 1614, fellow college of physicians 1651, until expelled 1681, librarian 1654-66, an original F.R.S.; died 19 Aug., 1695; buried in St. Andrew's, Holborn. See Ath. 430; & Munk's Roll, i. 258.

Godolphin, John, 3s. John, of the Scilly Isles, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 15; B.A. 15 Oct., 1635, B.C.L. 3 July, 1636, created D.C.L. March, 1642, or April, 1643, joint commissioner prerogative court of Canterbury 1641-58, and of court of admiralty 1653-9, a royal advocate; died 4 April, 1678. See Ath. iii. 1152; Coote's Civilians, 81; & D.N.B.

Allbright, William, s. Lawrence, of Mickleton, co. Glouc., pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll., 6 May, 1634, M.A. from Gloucester Hall 31 Jan., 1636-7, to officiate at Fryern Barnet, Herts, 18 April, 1646. See Add. MS., 15,670, p. 65.

Woodward, Cornelius, s. Richard, of St. Michael, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 16, B.A. 8 Dec., 1633; vicar of Lyminge, Kent, 1644, by the Westminster assembly of divines. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 97.

Sharsell, Nicholas, s. Nicholas, of Kilkhampton, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 19, B.A. 28 May, 1636, M.A. 1 Feb., 1638-9; vicar of Cubert or Cuthbert, Cornwall, 1646. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Albright, Robert (Allbright), s. William, of White, Wilts, 'sacerd.' Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov. 1632, aged 19; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 6 May, 1634, M.A. from Gloucester Hall 18 Feb., 1636-7.
Cory, Daniel (Corie), s. Daniel, of Bridgerule, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 22 (called Covy in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 4 June, 1635.
Prichard, Godwin, s. Edward, of Llantilyd, co. Glamorgan, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov. 1632, aged 19; B.A. 4 June, 1635, as Goodwyn.
Allen, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Elham, Kent, Sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 June, 1632, aged 16; B.A. from Merton Coll., 27 Feb., 1635-6.
Hutchings, Francis, s. Francis, of Morwinstow, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 18; brother of Thomas 1642.
Moulton, William, s. Charles, of Holliwell, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 20; B.A. 11 July, 1634.
Dobell, John, s. Thomas, of Collomb minor, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 25; called Doble in Mat. Reg.
Cleverdon, Antony, s. Antony, of Torrington Magna, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 20; B.A. 4 June, 1635.
Osborne, Matthew, s. Hugh, of Newton Ferrers, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Feb., 1632-3, aged 18; B.A. 15 Dec., 1638.
Tooker, Nicholas, s. Samuel, of Milton Abbot, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 21, B.A. 4 June, 1635.
Phayre, John (Phaer), s. George, of St. Keyne, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 19.
Martyne, Richard, s. William, of Tallant, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Feb., 1632-3, aged 19.
Spencer, Robert, s. Robert, of Purton, Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 15.
Philpott, James, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1632, aged 18; B.A. 6 Feb., 1632-3.

Bathurst, Ralph, s. George, of Coventry, co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1634, aged 14; scholar Trinity Coll. 1637, B.A. 27 Jan., 1637-8, fellow 1640, M.A., 17 April 1641, B. Med. 21 June, 1653, D. Med. 21 June, 1654, president 1664, vice-chancellor 1673, chaplain to Charles II., F.R.S., rector of Garsington, Oxon, 1664, dean of Wells 1670, refused the bishopric of Bristol, died 8 June, 1704, will proved at Oxford, 16 June, 1704. See Hearne, i. 231; Burrows; Fasti, ii. 183; Gutch, i. 533; & D.N.B.

Clifford, Anthony, born in Wilts, s. Simon, of Westminster, Middlesex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 June, 1634, aged 18, B.A. 24 Oct., 1637, M.A. 25 June, 1640; fellow of Exeter Coll. 1641-62, B.D. 2 Feb., 1652-3, delegate of the parliamentary visitors 1647, resigned 1662, rector of South Moreton, Berks, 1659, of Newton Ferrers, Devon, 1661, and of Aveton Gifford, Devon, 1674, died in 1685. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Burrows; & Boase, 67.

Lovelace, Richard, 1s. William, of Woolwich, Kent, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 June, 1634, aged 16; created M.A. 31 Aug., 1636, incorporated at Cambridge 1637, "afterwards eminent for his valour and poetry," spent all his estate in the royal cause and died in Gunpowder Alley, Shoe Lane; buried in St. Bride's 1658. See Berry's Kent, 475; Ath. iii. 460; & Fasti, i. 491.

Docton, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Hartland, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 18; of Hartland, aged 8 at the Heralds' Visitation in 1620, and baptized 26 Dec., 1611, buried at Hartland 17 Dec., 1663. See Vivian's Cornish Visitations.

Ley, Alexander, s. Thomas, of Monkleigh, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 26 May, 1638, vicar of Monkleigh 1648-73; father of the next-named, and of John 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Butler, John, s. John, of Penmark, co. Glamorgan, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 23; B.A. 24 Oct., 1637, his father, vicar of Penmark 1623. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Orcharde, Edmond, s. Walter, of St. Mary Weeke, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 16; B.A. 11 May, 1637, rector of Zeal Monachorum, Devon, 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bachler, John, s. John, of Burlingham, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634 aged 18; B.A. 31 Jan., 1636-7, M.A. 11 June, 1640, fellow of Eton 1647. See Fasti, i. 515.
Parsons, Bartholomew, s. Bar., of Lurgashall, Wilts, sac. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1634, aged 16; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 31 Jan., 1637-8; brother of John 1638.
Rouse, Bertram (Rowse), s. Oliver, of Kilkhampton, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 17; brother of Isaac, and William 1636.
Harvey, William s. Henry, of Winchcombe, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 15; B.A. 24 Oct., 1637. See Fasti, i. 496.
Hele, Arthur, s. John, of Dipford, Devon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1634, aged 19; of Dipford, baptised at Brixham 16 July, 1615.
Hardman, John, of Neen Sollers, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1634, aged 17; B.A. 15 Oct., 1636, M.A. 2 July, 1639.
Moore, Francis, s. Edmund, of Wareham, Dorset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 3 July, 1638. See Fasti, i. 500.
Bennett, Gilbert, s. Gilbert, of West Ansty, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 18. See Le Neve, iii. 192, 433.
Crymes, William, s. Philip, of Meavy, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 17; buried at Meavy 15 Oct., 1659.
Treweene, Degory, s. John, of Poundstock, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1634, aged 18, B.A. 20 April, 1638.
Watkins, John, s. John, of Stroud, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 18, B.A. 12 May, 1636.
Reynolds, Francis, s. William, of Kings Norton, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall., matric. 7 Nov., 1634, aged 18.
Diston, William, s. William, of Dumbleton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 18.
Arundell, Charles, s. George, of St. Mawgan, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 16.
Rundle, Nicholas, s. Nicholas, of Stratton, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 16.
Sage, Henry, s. John, of Weare Gifford, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 17.

Dyer, Edward, s. Edward, of Alaston, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 18; (called Edmund in Mat. Reg.) B.A. 16 May, 1639, perhaps vicar of Ilchester, Somerset, 1644-56 (as M.A.), father of James 1668. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Jackson, John, s. Thomas, of Mewall, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 18; B.A. 16 May, 1639. See Fasti, i. 507.
Blundell, Robert, s. Richard, of Barton Blunt, co. Derby, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 17; brother of Thomas 1635.
Blundell, Thomas, s. Richard, of Barton Blunt, co. Derby, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 15; brother of Robert 1635.
Maye, John, s. Thomas, of Bickley, co. Gloucester, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 15; B.A. 19 Feb., 1638-9.
Walter, John, s. Thomas, of Horfield, co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 June, 1635, aged 18.
Wilkes, Adam, s. Thomas, of Minton, Salop, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1635, aged 17.
Lush, Robert, s. John, of Donhead, Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1635, aged 16.

Aston, Antony, s. John, of Gretton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Oct., 1636, aged 17; B.A. 9 May, 1640, M.A. 1 Feb., 1642-3, rector of Little Comberton, co. Worcester, 1644. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
