Blagdon, Anthony (Blackdon), s. Antony, of Claughton, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 July, 1636, aged 18; B.A. 3 March, 1639-40. buried at Ashwater, Devon, 5 May, 1625.
Carpenter, Edward, s. Richard, of Sherwell, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 July, 1636, aged 16; B.A. 11 Feb., 1639-40.
Ley, Thomas, s. Thomas "Leigh," of Monkleigh, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 April, 1636, aged 17; B.A. 12 Dec., 1639.
Gibbes, William, s. George, of Bristol (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1636, aged 22.
Lee, James, s. Charles, of South Lea, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Feb., 1636-7, aged 17.

Carew, John, s. Richard, of East Antony, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1637-8, aged 15; the Regicide, admitted to the Inner Temple 1640, M.P. Tregony (Feb.) 1647-53, Devon 1653, Rumper 1659, one of the commissioners on trial of the king and signed the warrant, attainted and condemned to death 12 Oct., 1660, executed on the 15th at Charing Cross. See D.N.B. & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Butler, John, s. George, of St. Bride's, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1637-8, aged 18; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 9 Dec., 1641, rector of Loughor 1661, vicar of Pyle with Kenfig 1662, and rector of Colwinstone 1666, all in co. Glamorgan. See the following entry and Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Butler, Arnold, s. John, of St. Bride's, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1637-8. aged 17; B.A. 4 Dec., 1641, rector of Newton Nottage 1660, and of Loughor, co. Glamorgan, 1661, vicar of St. Bride's Major (also co. Glamorgan) 1661. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Whitaker, Henry, born in Dorset, s. William, of London, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 May, 1637, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1645, recorder of Shaftesbury and M.P. 1659 and 1661-79; died in 1696. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Carne, Edward, s. John, of Ewenny, co. Glamorgan, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1637-8, aged 14; of the Middle Temple in 1640, and migrated to Lincoln's Inn in 1673. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Parker, Richard, s. Edward, of Charlton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 March, 1637, aged 16; B.A. 22 March, 1640-1; perhaps rector of Cricksea, Essex, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Byker, John (Biker), s. John, of Dunchurch, co. Warwick, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 May, 1637, aged 17; a student of the Middle Temple 1638. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Lewes, James, s. George, of Buckland Filleigh, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 March, 1637-8, aged 18; B.A. 12 Oct., 1641.
Kinge, Matthew, s. John, of Shaston, Dorset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1637, aged 18; B.A. 8 July, 1641.
Highmore, James (Heighmore), s. Samuel, of Anderson, Dorset, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 May, 1637, aged 16.
Arundell, Francis, s. John, of Newland, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 March, 1637, aged 16.
Hulet, John, s. James, of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1637, aged 18.

Grevile, Samuel (Grivell), s. Francis, of Shaswell (Shottswell), co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 June, 1638, aged 17; B.A. 10 May, 1642, M.A. 3 Dec., 1646; perhaps fellow Trinity Coll. 1650 by the parliamentary visitors, vicar of Shottswell, co. Warwick, 1665. See Burrows, 554; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rashleigh, John, s. Jonathan, of Truro Drah, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 June, 1638, aged 17; of Menabilly; student of Lincoln's Inn 1640; baptised at Fowey 22 April, 1621, buried at Kenton, Devon, 18 July, 1651; father of the next and of Jonathan 1659. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Rous, Richard, s. William, of Halton, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1638, aged 17; of Halton, M.P. Bossiney, 1661, until his death, 1672-3; brother of Anthony 1633. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Cobb, William, 1s. William, of Adderbury, Oxon, equitis. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1638, aged 15; baptized at Adderbury 17 Nov., 1622, buried 10 April, 1639, brother of Arthur, Francis, and Thomas.

Collins, Francis (Collin), s. John, of Brenton, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 June, 1638, aged 16; B.A. 10 Feb., 1641-2, perhaps vicar of Charles, Devon, 1673. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Grenvile, Richard, s. Bevill, of Stow, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 April, 1638, aged 16; baptised at Tremere in Lanteglos 25 March, 1621; brother of John 1663, etc.
Phillipps, Edmond, s. Daniel, of Stratton, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 June, 1638, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1641. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Kelly, John, s. William, of Kelly, Devon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 April, 1638, aged 18; buried at Kelly 14 June, 1689.
Seys, William, s. Richard, of "Bodio," co. Glamorgan, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1638, aged 20.
Webber, Peter, s. Richard, of Bodmin, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 April, 1638, aged 17.

Wills, Francis, s. Francis, of St. Stevens, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 July, 1639, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1639, buried at St. Stephens 27 Jan., 1642-3; brother of Thomas 1639. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Fitzgeffry, Alexander, s. Charles, of St. Dominick, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 March, 1639-40, aged 21; one of these names vicar of Tamerton Foliatt, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Deeble, Nicholas, s. Nicholas, of Calstock, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 Dec., 1639, aged 19; B.A. 14 March, 1643-4, rector of Calstock 1645. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wheare, Samuel, s. Degory, principal of Gloucester Hall, Oxford, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 6 Dec., 1639, aged 14; vicar of Launcells, Cornwall, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wills, Thomas, s. Francis, of St. Stevens, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 July, 1639, aged 18; buried at St. Stephens by Saltash 13 Oct., 1663; brother of Francis 1639.
Prout, James, s. James, of North Petherwin, Devon, pleb. p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 Dec., 1639, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Lewannick, Cornwall, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gamage, Edmund, s. John, of St. Bride's, co. Glamorgan, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 June, 1639, aged 17; brother of Edward 1632, and perhaps father of Edmund 1670.
Rose, William, s. Christopher, of Bourton-underWater, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1639, aged 19, created M.A. 21 Feb., 1642-3.
Torr, Henry, s. Philip, of St. Probus, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 17, B.A. 21 Nov., 1643.
Jeffrey, John (Jeffery), s. Robert, of Mancoke Hampton, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 March, 1639-40, aged 17.
Teylder, Degory, s. William, of Trasmere, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 June, 1639, aged 20.
Dell, Richard, s. John, of Gerrans, Cornwall, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 17.
Hele, John, s. John, of Wisdom, Devon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 March, 1639-40, aged 15.

Wells, Michael, s. Francis, of Mickleton, co. Gloucester, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 27 Nov., 1640, aged 14, created M.A. 15 April, 1648; fellow of St. John's Coll. 1648, and of New Coll. 1649, by the visitors, subwarden 1654, an Arabic scholar; died 1685; brother of Francis 1627. See Burrows, 535, 550.
Holditch, John, s. John, of Combworthy, Devon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 March, 1640, aged 15.

Roberts, Richard, s. Robert, of Talyllyn, co. Anglesea, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 March, 1641-2, aged 15; B.A. from Christ Church 17 March, 1645-6; and perhaps M.A. 14 July, 1668; one of these names rector of Llanvetherine, co. Monmouth, 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ham, Richard (Hamb), s. William, of Milton, Devon, pleb., Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 March, 1641-2, aged 16; vicar of Ipplepen, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hutchins, Thomas, s. Francis, of Morewinstowe, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 March, 1641-2, aged 18; brother of Francis 1632.
Taylor, Thomas, s. Laurence, of Londesbrough, Yorks, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 21.
Bonython, Robert, s. Robert, of Thatcham, Berks, sacerd. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 18.
Parker, William, s. James, of Trengough, Cornwall, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 18.
Gibbon, John, s. John, of Tealby, co. Lincoln, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 14.
Cottell, Walter (Cottle), s. Walter, of Rosse, co. Wexford, Ireland, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 July, 1642, aged 15.
Raleigh, Joseph (Rawleigh), s. John, of Buckland Filleigh, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 May, 1642, aged 18.
Fox, Henry, s. Edmund, of Farmington, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 April, 1642, aged 19.
Chaunt, Francis, s. Thomas, of Chimney, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 April, 1642, aged 17.
Austin, Roger, s. Roger, of Lyfton, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 July, 1642, aged 18.
Fownes, Edward, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Nov., 1645, aged 17.

Bridges, Brooke, admitted to Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 26 Feb., 1647-8; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 20 March, 1649-50; fellow of New Coll. by visitors 1650, M.A. 15 July, 1652 (7s. John, of Hackney, bar.-at-law), (brother of Colonel John Bridges, governor of Warwick, etc.), commissioner of forfeited estates in Ireland, died at Holland House 1702. See Fasti, ii. 129; & Burrows.
Jeanes, Joseph, pleb., of Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 April, 1652, rector of Stanton Prior 1663, and of Blagdon, Somerset, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gaylard, John, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 April, 1652; one of these names vicar of Burnham, Somerset, 1654. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Studley, John, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1652, B.A. 10 June, 1653.
Wonaker, John, "ser." Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653 (subscribes "Wonneccer"); perhaps vicar of Angle or Nangle, co. Pembroke, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Figge, John (Fyge), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653, B.A. 5 March, 1656-7, vicar of Patrixbourn, Kent, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Trapp, Joseph, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1653; B.A. from Christ Church 26 Nov., 1656, M.A. 2 June, 1659.
Thomas, William, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653, B.A. 30 Jan., 1655-6. See Calamy, iii. 177.
Limbear, Christopher (Lymbear), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653.
Clapp, Richard, arm. fil. nat. max. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653.
Holworthy, William, "ser." Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 June, 1653.

Walton, Andrew, "serv." Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 July, 1654; scholar Wadham Coll. 1656, aged 18, from Higham, Somerset, B.A. 15 Feb., 1657-8; rector of East Mersea 1668, and of Ramsden Crays, (both) Essex, 1671. See Gardiner, 213; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Harborne, William, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1654.
Royse, Henry, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric: Michaelmas term 1657; B.A. 13 Oct., 1657.

Sabine, (Sir) John, arm. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn 1664 (as son and heir of John, of Beakesbourne, Kent, esq.); licenced 24 July, 1665, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of William Allen, of Eyne in the parish of Gravenhurst, Beds, spinster; knighted 22 March, 1670-1. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Jones, Caleb, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9; fellow Corpus Christi Coll., B.A. 24 Feb., 1662-3, M.A. 15 March, 1665-6, B.D. 1674; died 3 Aug., 1675, aged 34, buried in college chapel, will at Oxford 1 June, 1675. See Gutch, i. 407.

Chetham, James, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1658, perhaps of the Middle Temple 1662, as son and heir of George, of Turton, co. Lancaster, perhaps father of James and Samuel. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Oldham, Abel, "ser." Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1658; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 6 March, 1660-1.
Granger, Lancelot (Grandger), gent. Gloucester Hall. matric. 10 Dec., 1658.
Harison, Phinehas, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1658.

Vale, John, s. William, of Coventry, co. Warwick, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Dec., 1660, aged 19; vicar of Wolfhamcote 1666, and of Napton-on-the-Hill, co. Warwick, 1676. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bessey, Thomas, of Middlesex, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1660; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 1666.
Trap(p), William, cler. fil. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 June, 1660.

Prichard, Hugh, s. Richard Hughes, of Clynnog, co. Carnarvon, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1661, aged 20, B.A. 1665; one of these names rector of Normanton-on-Soar, Notts, 1679. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Avant, Philip, s. Philip, of 'St. Budokes,' Cornwall, pleb., p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 19; vicar of Salcombe, Devon, 1666. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wooford, Henry, s. Thomas, of Oxford, pleb. p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Dec., 1661, aged 17; perhaps rector of Elvetham, Hants, 1690-1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hull, William, s. Thomas, of Southstoke, Somerset, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1661, aged 24; inventory at Oxford 11 July, 1662.
Higgs, Jeremiah, s. Edmund, of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 July, 1661, aged 28; B.Med. 12 Sept. 1661.
Griffith, John, s. "Griff Owen", of Keidio, co. Carnarvon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 June, 1661, aged 21.
Waller, John, s. Leon, of Cransford, Suffolk, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 20.
Plant, Robert, s. Joh., of Toddenham, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 Dec., 1662, aged 16.

Gregory, Isaac, s. Joshua, of Okehampton, Devon, pleb. pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 16; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1666, M.A. 1669, rector of Binstead, Isle of Wight, 1681, vicar of Peterborough St. John 1698; brother of John same date. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Warren, Robert, s. Rob., of Rame, Cornwall, minister, p.p. (subscribes "paup. fil."). Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 March, 1663, aged 17; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1666; rector of Rame 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gregory, John, s. Joshua, of Okehampton, Devon, pleb. pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 17; perhaps rector of Dodbrooke, Devon, 1682; brother of Isaac same date. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hardinge, John, s. Edw., of Elmly Lovett, co. Worcester, pleb. pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 June, 1663, aged 19.
Whitefoote, Decimus, s. Tho., of Worcester (city), minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1663, aged 16.
Jago, George, s. John, of Llanteglos, Cornwall, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 Nov., 1663, aged 16.

Durnford, Joseph, s. Rich., of New Sarum, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1664, aged 17, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1667; incorp. at Cambridge 1671, M.A. from Catharine Hall, Cambridge, 1671, rector of Orchardleigh, Somerset, 1674, and vicar of Whitsbury, Hants, 1676, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Williams, Charles, s. Tho., of Gwythelwern, co. Merioneth, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 19, B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671; rector of Twickenham, Middlesex, 1687-1707. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Horsman, Nicholas, s. Nic., of Plimpton, Devon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 30; student of Middle Temple 1659. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gilbert, Richard, s. John, of Launceston, Cornwall, pleb. pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1664, aged 19.
Dowrishe, Samuel, s. Sam., of Calstock, Cornwall, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1665, aged 18; rector of St. Keyne, Cornwall, 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Phillips, Henry, s. Sam., of Whilton, Northants, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 April, 1666, aged 16; B.A. 1669, rector of Whilton 1674, until his death; buried there 11 Aug., 1684; his father rector 1644, until buried there 21 March, 1670-1. See Baker's Northants, i. 234; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Kinge, Robert, s. Joseph, of Modbury, Devon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 April, 1666, aged 16; one of these names vicar of Colyton Rawleigh, Devon, 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Daniell, John, s. Max, of St. Agnes, Cornwall, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 July, 1666, aged 20; vicar of St. Constantine, Cornwall, 1674. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Price, William, s. Rice, of Llanrhaiader, co. Montgomery, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 March, 1666-7, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 1670.
Saunders, John, s. John, of Colton, co. Stafford, minister, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1666-7, aged 19.
Fournesse, Ichabod, s. Tob., of Manchester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 April, 1666, aged 15; B.A. 1669.

Lees, Thomas, s. Tho., of Hulme, co. Lancaster, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 June, 1667, aged 19; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1671, M.A. 1676, rector of Goodneston, Kent, 1678, and vicar of Graveney, Kent, 1680; father of Thomas 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Furse, William (Fursse), s. Tho., of Evercreech, Somerset, minister, pp. (subs. paup. fil.). Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1667, aged 18; rector of Holcombe, Somerset, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Conwey, Hugh, s. William, of Abererch, co. Carnarvon, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 March, 1667-8, aged 15; B.A. 1671, vicar of Abererch 1673, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thorne, Daniel, s. Rich., of Tiverton, Devon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 May, 1667, aged 18; rector of Templeton, Devon, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, William, s. William, of Dowdeswell, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 March, 1667-8, aged 17. See Robert 1685.
Rutland, Edmund, s. Edm., of Westbury, co. Gloucester, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 July, 1667, aged 16, B.A. 1671, M.A. 1674.
Cole, Thomas, s. Tho., of Duntsborne Abbots, co. Gloucester, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 March, 1667-8, aged 18.
Thomas, Charles, s. Dav., of Leicester (town), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 March, 1667-8, aged 20, B.A. 1670.
Atkins, Richard, s. Rich., of Llansanfrayd, co. Montgomery, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 July, 1667, aged 20.
Robinson, Robert, s. Edm., of Clitheroe, co. Lancaster, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 March, 1667-8, aged 19.
Evans, John, s. Bonner, of Plasdu, co. Carnarvon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 March, 1667-8, aged 16.

Owens, Humphrey, s. "Owen Roberts," of Llannorin-Llun, co. Carnarvon, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1668, aged 18; perhaps rector of Bettws-Gwerfyl-Goch, co. Merioneth, 1683; perpetual curate of Llangystenyn 1683, and vicar of Llanbeblick, co. Carnarvon, 1684. See Thomas, 558, and Foster's Index Eccl.

Cawthorne, Richard, s. William, of Warwick (town), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 May, 1668, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 11 March, 1671-2; incorp. at Cambridge 1683, M.A. from Trinity Coll. 1683, vicar of Hampton-on-Thames 1679. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Standfast, John, s. Rich., of Bristol, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1668-9, aged 16; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 1672, M.A. 1675; rector of Portishead 1682, and of Weston-inGordano, Somerset, 1684. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Browning, Richard, s. Edw., of Dursley, co. Gloucester, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 May, 1668, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 27 Jan., 1671-2, vicar of Ile Abbots, Somerset, 1673.
Jenkins, John, s. William, of Morton, co. Hereford, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1668-9, aged 18; B.A. 1672, M.A. 1675.
Jones, David, s. "Joh. Williams", of Llanuffydd, co. Denbigh, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 July, 1668, aged 19; B.A. 1672.
Jones, Humphrey, s. Rog., of Llanrhayader, co. Montgom., pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 March, 1668-9, aged 18, B.A. 1672.
Ousley, John (Owsley), s. Joh., of Glooston, co. Lincoln, minister. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 March, 1668-9, aged 14.
Blisse, John, s. Anth., of Painswick, co. Gloucester, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 July, 1668, aged 18; B.A. 1672.
Swayn, Richard (Swain), s. John, of Towyn, co. Merioneth, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 July, 1668, aged 16.
Clifford, James, s. Edw., of Oxford (city), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 July, 1668, aged 47.
Turner, Thomas, s. William, of Bristol, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1668-9, aged 16.

Geddes, Michael, s. Rob., of "Cornhill in Marcy's Scot," gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1669, aged 22; M.A. Edinburgh University 1668, incorporated from Balliol Coll. 11 July, 1671, LL.D. Lambeth 16 April, 1695, chancellor of Salisbury cathedral 1691, chaplain to the English factory in Portugal 1678-87, rector of Farmington, co. Gloucester, 1688-93, of Compton Bassett, Wilts, 1693, and of East Hendred, Berks, 1710, until his death 12 April, 1713; father of the last-named. See Chalmers' Dictionary, xv. 378-9; Fasti, ii. 330; Rawl. i. 219, xvii. 161; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.

Browne, Christopher, s. Anthony, of Bristol, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1669, aged 17, B.A. 1673; licenced 23 June, 1679 (then of Pucklechurch, co. Gloucester), to marry Elizabeth, daughter of William Hall, rector of Avening same county rector of Priston, Somerset, 9 July, 1679. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Parker, Edward, s. Richard, of Clifton, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 July, 1669, aged 17; B.A. 1673, rector of Rowberrow, Somerset, 1677, and vicar of Bitton, co. Gloucester, 1691. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Widdowes, Jervis, s. Tho., of Northleach, co. Gloucester, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 April, 1669, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 1672; rector of Great Kimble, Bucks, 1683. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Stephenson, William (Stevenson), s. Tho., of Sanderton, Bucks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 18, B.A. 1672, M.A. 1675; vicar of Bledlow, Bucks, 1678. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Footman, Thomas, s. Rich., of Llansaintffraid, co. Montgomery, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1669, aged 20; B.A. 1672, vicar of Little Horwood, Bucks, 1676. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bagwell, John, s. John, of Truro, Cornwall, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 18; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1672, M.A. from New Inn Hall 1676.
Samborne, Thomas, s. Mawdley, of Timsborough, Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Aug., 1669, aged 18; brother of William 1680.
Holdway, William (Houldway), s. William, of Ember, Wilts, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 July, 1669, aged 17; B.A. 1673.
Jones, Edward, s. "Joh. Evans", of Spytty, co. Carnarv., pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 21.
Moris, Robert, s. Tho., of Pennant, co. Montgomery, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 25 Feb., 1669-70, aged 20.
Goddard, Daniel, s. Ph., of Ottery St. Mary, Devon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 July, 1669, aged 17.
Birt, Thomas, s. Tho., of Bristol, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 July, 1669, aged 18; B.A. 1673.

Vaughan, Richard, s. John, of Llangibby, co. Carnarvon, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1670, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1680, treasurer elect 1716, (as son of John, of Derllis, co. Carmarthen, esq.), M.P. Carmarthen in 14 parliaments, 1685-7, 1689, until his death in 1724 (dead before 10 Feb., 1725-6). See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Collier, David, of Farington, Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 May, 1670, aged 17; B.A. 28 Feb., 1673-4, born at Great Faringdon in June, 1652, vicar of Coxwell, Berks, 1676, died there 21 Oct., 1724. See Rawlinson, v. 47, xvi. 410; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Singleton, Thomas, s. Laur., of Gloucester (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 July, 1670, aged 17; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1674; M.A. from Hart Hall 1677, rector of Wootton, Northants, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Nic(h)olson, William, of Gloucester (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1670, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll, 1671-8, B.A. 1674, M.A. 1677; died 15 Dec., 1678. See Gutch, i. 343; & Bloxam, v. 312.

Combstocke, Thomas, s. Th., of 'Curreele' (Curry Rivell), Somerset, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1670, aged 19; rector of Weston Bampfield, Somerset, 1681. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Herbert, Watkin, s. W., of Cardigan, pleb. (subs. pp.). Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1670, aged 18; brother of Thomas same date.
Herbert, Thomas, s. W., of Cardigan, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1670, aged 17; brother of "Watkin" same date.
Powell, Humphrey, s. Phil., of Gwyddelwern, co. Merioneth, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1670, aged 18.
Williams, William, s. Ezek., of Trefdaeth, co. Anglesea, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1670, aged 18.
Lloyd, Evan, s. Hen., of Bettws, co. Denbigh, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1670, aged 21.

Jones, John, s. John, of Aberfraw, co. Anglesey, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 June, 1671, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 1674, perhaps rector of Llandegvan, co. Anglesey, 1683, of Heneglwys 1694, and of Aberfraw 1694. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, Roger, s. "Joh. Thomas", of Gwyddelwern, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 March, 1671-2, aged 16; B.A. 1675, vicar of Gwyddelwern 1676-84. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bennett, Stephen, s. Steph., of St. Gerran's, Cornwall, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 June, 1671, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 23 Jan., 1674-5, brother of John same date.
Sparke, Thomas, s. Tobias, of Hawkswick, Yorks, p.p. (subs. "pleb."). Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1671, aged 24.
Griffiths, Isaac, s. David, of Llandewy, co. Radnor, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 June, 1671, aged 18.
Hughes, Matt, s. M., of "Bristow," pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 2 June, 1671, aged 17.
Morris, John, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 7 April, 1671, aged 18.
Robertes, Moses, s. Griffin, of Llandwyey, co. Merioneth, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1672-3, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Lanilar, co. Cardigan, 1692. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Emott, Philologus, s. Bryan, of Bledlow, Bucks, pp. (subs. as "populus filius"). Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1672, aged 19.
Jones, Rowland, s. "Johan David", of Malloyd, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 21 March, 1672-3, aged 21.
Cooper, John, s. Edward, of Butley, co. Chester, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 July, 1672, aged 18; B.A. 1676.
Mathew, Edward, s. "Mat. Jones," of Anglesey, (town), p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 March, 1672, aged 20.
Lewis, Peter, s. Steph., of Gwyddelwern, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 May, 1672, aged 20.
Sugden, William, s. Joh., of Carlton-in-Craven, Yorks, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 May, 1672, aged 20.
Hunt, Daniel, s. John, of Bisney, co. Gloucester, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 3 July, 1672, aged 18.
Coles, Saunders, s. Edw., of Oxford (city), pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 March, 1672, aged 15.
Gilbody, Benjamin, s. R., of "Lym," Cheshire, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 May, 1672, aged 22.
Lloyd, Thomas, s. Joh., of Anglesey (town), pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 29 March, 1672, aged 17.
Jones, Thomas, s. Andr., of Tredunock, co. Monmouth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 May, 1673, aged 19; subs. as of Jesus Coll., and B.A. 28 Feb., 1677-8, M.A. 1680.
Huggett, Anthony, s. A., of Chaldon, Surrey, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1673, aged 19; of Cambridge university 6 July, 1671.
Griffithes, Robert, s. Joh., of Mallwyd, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 May, 1673, aged 24; called "Griffin" in Mat. Reg.
Rhodes, Thomas, s. William, of Burford, Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1673, aged 17; brother of John 1664.
Jones, William, s. "Joh. Thomas", of Mallwyd, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 June, 1673, aged 21.
Lloyd, Owen, s. John, of Llangwnadle, co. Carnarvon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1674, aged 16 (as Floyd); B.A. from St. Mary Hall 28 Feb., 1677-8, M.A. 1686 as Lloyd.
Floyd, Owen, s. Joh., of Llangwnadle, co. Carnarvon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1674, aged 16, as Lloyd; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 28 Feb., 1677-8, M.A. 1680.
Smith, John, s. George, of Hilmorton, co. Warwick, p.p. Gloucester Hall., matric. 30 Oct., 1674, aged 19.

Griffith, Evan, s. "Griffin Jones", of Beth Kellet, co. Caernarvon, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 26 March, 1675, aged 17; B.A. from New Inn Hall 1678, M.A. 1681 (as Griffiths); perhaps rector of Llanfachraeth, co. Anglesey, 1699, and of Llangyber, co. Carnarvon, 1702. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Peboth, "Hieremias,", s. Hier., of Newbury, Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1677, aged 21.
Price, John, s. And., of Oxford (city), cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 April, 1677, aged 17.

Brideoak, Jethro, s. Jabez, of Shalbourne, Berks, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 May, 1679, aged 16; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1683, M.A. 1686, his father instituted vicar of Shalbourne, Berks, 17 June, 1661, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Mallory, Thomas, s. Tho., of Mobberley, Cheshire, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 April, 1679, aged 19; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1682, rector of Mobberley 1684, deprived 1690; born 3 June, 1657; died 1713.

Bowber, Robert, s. R., of Sandford, Devon, paup. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1680, aged 19; B.A. from New Inn Hall 1684, M.A. 1687, vicar of Truro St. Mary 1692, and rector of Strattonon-the-Fosse, Somerset, 1693. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Eaton, Edward, s. Byrom, of Oxford city, S.T.D. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 April, 1680, aged 14; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1683. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gruchy, Martin, s. M., of the Isle of Jersey, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1680, aged 14.
Payn, Thomas, s. Philip, of Jersey, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 17 Dec., 1680, aged 13.
Burges, Robert, s. Robert, of Wallingford, Berks, paup. Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 July, 1681, aged 18; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 1686.
Dalgarno, George, s. Geo., of Oxford (city), cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 March, 1688-9, aged 22; admitted to practice in surgery 5 Dec., 1689; for a biographical notice of his father see D.N.B.

Dubourdieu, John Armand, of Montpelier, France, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1693-4, aged 16; chaplain to the factory at Smyrna, a minister of the Episcopal French Church, Savoy, chaplain to the Duke of Devonshire, rector of Sawtrey Moyns, Hunts, 1701, died at his lodgings in Green Street, near Leicesterfields, 25 March, 1727, buried 27th, in St. Martins-in-the-Fields churchyard, known for his services against popery and the pretender. See Rawlinson, i. 410, iii. 214, 253, xvii. 65.

Woodroffe, Benjamin, s. B., of Gresham college, London, doctoris. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 14 Feb., 1693-4, aged 10; B.A. from Christ Church 1697, M.A. 1700; vicar of Frodisham, Cheshire, 1711, canon of Worcester 1722, rector of Harvington, co. Worcester, 1725, canon of Winchester 1726, and rector of Over Wallop 1730-70, and of Cliddesden with Farleigh, (both) Hants, 1760, until his death 5 Aug., 1770. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Stonehouse, (Sir) George, s. Joh., of Amerden Hall, Essex, bart. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1693-4, aged 15; 6th bart., died 13 April, 1695.
Bourdieu, John Armand Du, of 'Montpelier,' France, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1693-4, aged 16.

Kinnersley, Thomas, s. William, of Ludlow, co. Hereford, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Nov., 1694, aged 15; B.A. 1698, M.A. 1701, incorporated at Cambridge 1714, rector of Lavenham, Suffolk, 1710, minister of Trinity Minories, "a bad lot." See Rawl. ii. 65, xx. 315; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Coxeter, Benjamin, s. Ric., of Brampton, Oxon, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 May, 1694, aged 11; B.A. 1 March, 1697-8, M.A. 1700, brother of Richard and Thomas.
Jones, Hugh, s. Tho., of Llanendwin, co. Merioneth, pp. Gloucester Hall, matric. 15 Nov., 1694, aged 23.

Scudamore, James, born at Shannon Park, Ireland, 1s. John, viscount. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 25 Sept., 1695, aged 11; created D.C.L. 12 May, 1712, then 3rd viscount Scudamore; M.P. Herefordshire in 3 parliaments 1705-13, and Hereford 1715, until he died 2 Dec., 1716. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Hartman, Samuel, s. Paul, of Shillingford, Berks, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1695, aged 15; B.A. from Christ Church 1699, M.A. 1703, petty (or minor) canon; vicar of Daventry, Northants, 1707, and of Badby 1708, until his death; buried there 22 June, 1716. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, John, s. Cadwal., of Greenar, Devon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1695, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1695 (his father, of Greenham, Somerset, esq.), perhaps rector of Huntsham, Devon, 1704. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Twysden, Heneage, s. William, of Peckham, Kent, bart. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1695-6, aged 15; B.A. from Christ Church 1701, M.A. 11 March, 1702-3; fell at the battle of Malplaquet 11 Sept., 1709.
Bouchier, Roger, s. Thomas, of Oxford (city), p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1695, aged 14; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1702. [Addenda: an original fellow of Worcester Coll. 1714. See Gutch, i. 631.]
Clarke, Thomas, s. James, of 'Fitz,' Salop, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1695, aged 18; rector of Fitz 1703, his father instituted to Fitz in 1661. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Baldwyn, Edwyn, s. Stephen, of Twinning, co. Gloucester, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1695, aged 14; a student of the Middle Temple 1696. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Lowndes, Robert, s. William, of Westmorland, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 14 July, 1695, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1696. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Russell, William, s. Joh., of Southwark, Surrey, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1695, aged 17; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1699.
Pilkington, John, s. Hug., of Budaston, co. Lancaster, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1695, aged 21.
Rouse, John, s. Joh., of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1695, aged 15.

Harcourt, Philip, y.s. Phil, of "Stanton Harcourt," Oxon, equitis. Gloucester Hall, matric. 31 March, 1696, aged 15; of Wigsell, a student of the Inner Temple 1694; died in 1708; half-brother of Simon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
James, John, s. John, of Latimers, Bucks, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 March, 1696-7, aged 16; B.A. 1700, M.A. 1703, rector of Weeley, Essex, 1700-2. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Haines, George, s. Ric., of Fairford, co. Gloucester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 22 March, 1696-7, aged 17; B.A. 1701.

Guise, Samuel, s. Tho., of Burcester, Oxon, pleb. (fellmonger). Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1697-8, aged 15; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1713; born at Burcester 24 March, 1681; master of the free school Chipping Wycombe, Bucks, 1707, vicar of Tame 1711, chaplain to Philip, Duke of Wharton, 1719. See Rawl. i. 296, xvii. 192: & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Harrison, Sedgwick, s. Lancelot, of London, doctoris, (subs.) "Med. Doc." Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1697, aged 14, B.A. 1701, M.A. 1705; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1706, B.C.L. 10 March, 1706-7, D.C.L. 1710, Camden professor of ancient history 1720-7; brother of Lancelot 1692. See Hearne, i. 301; & Rawl. i. 291, v. 96.

Ryley, Reginald, s. Phil., of London, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 July, 1697, aged 16; his father serjeant-at-arms 1685-1702, 1706-28, and knighted 26 April, 1728—he was serjeant-at-arms 1702-6; buried at Hockham, Norfolk, 29 Dec., 1728. See Water's Chesters of Chicheley, i. 179.

Hughes, George, s. William, of Worcester city, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1697, aged 16; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704; one of these names rector of Oxwick 1712, and of Colkirk St. Mary, Norfolk, 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Werden, (Sir) John, s, Joh., of London, bart. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1697-8, aged 15; 2nd bart., student of Inner Temple 1700, died 13 Feb., 1758. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pearse, William, s. G. (? " Gul."), of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1697, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1703, as of London, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Methwold, William, s. Tho., of Brompton, Middle sex, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1697-8, aged 15; of St. Giles, admon. to his son Thomas granted in Jan., 1743.
Guise, John, s. William, of Oxford (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 6 July, 1697, aged 14; possibly identical with the next man, migrated to Merton Coll.
Andrews, Nich, s. Sam., of Wantage, Berks, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1697-8, aged 16; B.A. 15 March, 1702-3, vicar of Great Bedwin, Wilts, 1715.
Burrington, Gilbert, s. John, of Sandford, Devon, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1697-8, aged 15; buried at Sandford 5 May, 1703.

Callow, Joseph, s. Benjamin, of Stow, co. Gloucester, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 Feb., 1698-9, aged 18; B.A. 1702, vicar of Stanway, co. Gloucester, 1706, brother of Benjamin 1687. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Murray, James, s. John, Earl of Dunmore, in Scotland. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1698, aged 15; Viscount Fincastle (s. Charles, Earl of Dunmore); died 24 July, 1706. See Foster's Peerage.
Loveday, Joseph, s. James, of Bradenham, Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1698, aged 19; B.A. 1702, rector of Hedsor, Bucks, 1715, and of Taplow 1723. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wallop, Bluet, s. Joh., of Husbands, Hants, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1698, aged 15; died in 1707.
Quarells, George, s. Ric., of Taunton, Somerset, p.p. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1698, aged 19.

Owen, Robert, s. Rob., of Hereford (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 March, 1699-1700, aged 22; B.A. 9 Dec., 1700; perhaps M.A. Cambridge 1728; perhaps rector of Partney and of Mablethorpe St. Peter 1728, Theddlethorpe St. Helen 1732, and of Belleau 1738, all co. Lincoln. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bathurst, Charles, s. Edward, of Lechlade, co. Gloucester, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 20; B.A. 1703, died unmarried, his father became 3rd baronet. See Rudder's Gloucestershire, 354.
Davis, Edward, s. E(dward), of Tainton, Oxon, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 March, 1699-1700, aged 17; B.A. 1703, M.A. 1706, his father instituted to vicarage of Taynton 5 Dec., 1681.
Taylor, Thomas, s. Th., of Preston, co. Lancaster, gent. — of Campden, co. Gloucester, in 4to. — subscribes "pleb." Gloucester Hall, matric. 4 May, 1700, aged 15, B.A. 1706.
Alexander, Joseph, s. J., of Bromsgrove, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 12 July, 1700, aged 15; B. A. 1704. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Harris, Chas, s. Ric. (C., in 4to.), of Wiccham, Bucks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 Oct., 1700, aged 16 ("19" in 4to.).
Groguet, Isaac, s. Fran., of "Galli," cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 1 July, 1700, aged 16.

Debat, Daniel, s. James, of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1702-3, aged 15; B.A. 1706, M.A. 1710, rector of Holdenby, Northants, 1730-9, preb. of Heredum-Marney in Collegiate Church of Endellion, Cornwall, 1738, rector of Reppingale, co. Lincoln, 1738. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Griffiths, Morris, s. Edw., of Marston Trussell, Northants, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1702, aged 17; B.A. 1706 (as Maurice), subs. as son of Edward, of Berriew, co. Montgomery, pleb., perhaps vicar of Berriew, co. Montgomery, 1719. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Sterpin, John, s. Fran., of London, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1702-3, aged 15, B.A. 1706; M.A. from Queen's Coll. 1709; rector of Denham, Bucks, 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Devereux, Edward, s. Vaughan, of Berry, co. Montgomery, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 11 Feb., 1702-3, aged 19; an uncle of Edward, Viscount Hereford 1748.
Jouneau, Daniel, s. Phil., of "Galliae," cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1702-3, aged 18.
Walmsley, Jeffery, s. Henry, of Mellar, co. Lancaster, cler. Gloucester Hall. matric. 13 May, 1703, aged 16, B.A. 20 Jan., 1706-7: vicar of Brafferton, Yorks, 1711. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Whitaker, John, s. J., of Welford, Northants, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1703, aged 19.
Pym, William, s. William (4to.), of London, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 Dec., 1704, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1703, etc.; brother of John 1705. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Pym, John, s. William, of London, arm. Gloucester Hall, matric. entry 24 May, 1705, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1716, as 2s. William, of Norton Bury, Herts, esq.; brother of William 1704. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Giberne, "Daniel Gabriel," s. Reginald, of London, Middlesex, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1706, aged 19, B.A. 1710 (as 'Gabell Gifford'); M.A. from Trinity Hall., Cambridge, 1713, canon of Lincoln 1722, rector of Ashley, co. Stafford, 1731. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, Cadwallader, s. Cadwallader, of Camley, Somerset, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 May, 1706, aged 17; B.A. 1710; rector of Ubley, Somerset, 1716. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wheeler, John, s. John, of North Morton, Berks, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1706, aged 16, B.A. 9 March, 1710-11; brother of Devereux.

Lloyd, John, s. James, of Clapham, Sussex, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 July, 1707, aged 16; migrated to Brasenose Coll. 1707, B.A. 1711, M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1724, rector of Maresfield, Sussex, 1728, and vicar of Chiddingley, 1737; his father vicar of Rustington, and rector of Clapham, Sussex, 1691. See Rawl. iv. 168; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Williams, Owen, s. William, of Llandrillo, co. Denbigh, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Feb., 1707-8, aged 19; B.A. from Christ Church 12 March, 1711-12; perhaps head master of Llanrwst school 1712, and rector of Llysfaen 1726, and of Llanrug, (both) co. Carnarvon, 1726. See Thomas, 568; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Gambier, James, s. Nic., of Westminster (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 18 March, 1707-8, aged 16; B.A. 29 Feb., 1711-12, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1717. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Barney, Francis, s. Joh., of King's Norton, co. Worcester, cler. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Feb., 1707-8, aged 17; B.A. 1711, M.A. 1714.
Foley, James, s. John, of Davenham, Cheshire. doctoris. Gloucester Hall, matric. 24 Oct., 1707, aged 16.

Norton, Thomas, s. Ralph, of Wallingford, Berks, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 9 July, 1709, aged 22; perhaps rector of Wallingford SS. Leonard and Peter 1722, and S. Mary 1728; brother of Joseph 1714. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sayer, Joseph, s. Pet., of Wallingford, Berks, gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1709-10, aged 15, B.A. 1713; M.A. from Worcester Coll. 1717, B.Med. 1719.
Strode, George, s. George, of Westminster (city), Middx., gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 16 June, 1709, aged 26.

Lacy, William, s. G. (? Gul.), of Bromsgrove, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 27 Jan., 1712-13, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 1716, M.A. 1720, rector of St. George-superEly 1717, and of St. Bride with St. Michael-superEly (all co. Glamorgan), 1720. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Parsons, Joseph, s. Dan., of Wallingford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1712, aged 14; B.A. from Merton Coll. 1716, M.A. 1718, rector of Hempnall 1724, and vicar of Bedingham, Norfolk, 1725, until his death 11 July, 1774. See Gutch MS. 223a; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Herbert, Matthew, s. William, of Woodstock, Oxon, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 10 April, 1712, aged 17; B.A. from Worcester Coll. 1715, M.A. 1718, rector of Somerton, Suffolk, 1724, and of Castle Combe, Wilts, 1728. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lamb, Francis, s. F., of Atterbury, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1712, aged 20; vicar of Elmley Castle, co. Worcester, 1728. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ravenhill, Thomas, s. Joh., of Hereford (city), gent. Gloucester Hall, matric. 20 March, 1712-13, aged 17; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1716, M.A. 1719.

Greenaway, William, s. William, of Sarum, Wilts, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 13; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 1716, M.A. 1719, chaplain to Lord Talbot, son of the chancellor; rector of St. Martin's in Salisbury 1727, and vicar of Nether Wallop, Hants, 1729, until his death 18 Aug. (or Oct.), 1734. See Rawl. vi. 152, xvii. 184; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Pol(e)hampton, John, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 17 March, 1713-14; B.A. from Worcester Coll. 1718, rector of Halford, co. Warwick, 1719. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Henley, James, s. J., of Kidderminster, co. Worcester, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 5 Feb., 1713-14; aged 17.
Jones, John, s. Tho., of Goodrich, co. Hereford, pleb. Gloucester Hall, matric. 19 March, 1713-14, aged 19.
